text: Mlabitɛ eboa la gi aabasɛ si mlekyi ní mlɔ ina nɛ mɛ ɛ.
translated: The children who came yesterday are not the ones I saw.

ref: Then a voice from heaven said, “This is my own dear Son, and I am pleased with him.”
text: Lɛ ole ɔtɔ ɛpani dɔni lifunu ne si, “Mobi ye liɛ ya yɛ madimɛ nɔ yɛ liboe liku me aŋu u!”
translated: "And when the woman saw that it was good, she said, 'Now I will eat it and give some to my husband!'"

ref: But instead of staying in Nazareth, Jesus moved to Capernaum. This town was beside Lake Galilee in the territory of Zebulun and Naphtali.
text: Asɛ ní Nazaret lɛ adzɛ eze ní Kapernaum, gi onu ɔma ɔtɔ ní Galilea limu ne kutu ní Zebulon ni Naftali kuseo me e.
translated: He left Nazaret and went to live in Capernaum, a town in the region of Galilee located in the territories of Zebulun and Naphtali.

ref: Then the devil came to him and said, “If you are God's Son, tell these stones to turn into bread.”
text: Lɛ Ozimizi ye aba yɛ klɔ ɔ; lɛ asi yɛ si, “Xe Aya Yebi wonu i, kɔlɔ ki tɔ akla la ya edzì bidɔŋaŋa aki wɔ.”
translated: When Ozimizi came to him; when he said to him, "The Lord has sent me, so that you may receive your sight and be filled with the Holy Spirit."

ref: Jesus walked on until he saw James and John, the sons of Zebedee. They were in a boat with their father, mending their nets. Jesus asked them to come with him.
text: Yesu atrɛ ní ɔtɔ nɔ lɛ azɛɛmɔ beneminyime tiaʋa dzedze e. Ba beze Yakobo ni Yohanes, gi benu Zebedeo yɛ bebi ba. Beze ní ba katapokpa me ni ba ɔkaɛ gi bɛzɛlulɔ ba dzikle ye e. Niteme si Yesu axwa ba kyɛ.
translated: Jesus called to him those whom he wanted to be his twelve apostles. Among them were James and John, who were the sons of Zebedee. He said that they should be with him and that he would send them to preach his word. This is what Jesus did for them.

ref: and he taught them:
text: Lɛ ekpese ba bidɔ kubasɛ si,
translated: When the time comes for them to harvest,

ref: God blesses those people who grieve. They will find comfort!
text: Isami iku banama gi bɛwa awowoe la ɛ, lɛ ese linu si Aya aayɔ ba ina a.
translated: When Isami's children were still small, she used to go to Aya's place to greet her.

ref: You are the light for the whole world. A city built on top of a hill cannot be hidden,
text: “Mlalɛ ŋwisi kani aki kelea mɛ. Ɔma gi bɛtsa ní lito aʋa ɛ, óʋikpo o.
translated: The child does not want to eat food. If you force him to eat it, he will vomit.

ref: The earth is God's footstool, so don't swear by the earth. Jerusalem is the city of the great king, so don't swear by it.
text: Mlakítani kesea, lɛ ese linu si ka kenu Aya yɛ ikli lɛ odu klɔ ɔ. Mlakítani Yerusalem kyɛ ɛ lɛ ese linu si onu Okusi ʋidi ye yɛ ɔma.
translated: The Samaritans believed that the place where people should worship God was on their mountain. The people of Jerusalem said that the place where God should be worshipped was in Jerusalem.

ref: Before you are dragged into court, make friends with the person who has accused you of doing wrong. If you don't, you will be handed over to the judge and then to the officer who will put you in jail.
text: “Xe ɔnutɔ akɔ liboe ní wɔ su ní otsixwakɔ ɔ, xe lipoe le lilɛ ɛ, ye liboe le nì yɛ xe litsixwawe le liana a. Xegi aní nite e, xe mleku otsixwaklɔ ɛ, aakɔ̀ wɔ kukpɛ awla ki otsixwa yɛ, kɔ yɛ kyɛ akɔ wɔ akpɛ awla aki bewukpɛ ʋa ɛ. Niteme si biakpɛ wɔ ligba.
translated: When someone wants to marry a woman, if the woman agrees, they will take her to the chief's house. After that, when the marriage ceremony is performed, they will give her gifts and marry her, and she will then give birth to children for him. These are the things they do at home.

ref: I promise you will not get out until you have paid the last cent you owe. Marriage
text: Sugba sugba ɛ, níklɔ wɔaze kɔkpɛni lipoe lɛ gi wɔatsa liʋɛ kakaa gi bɛnya ɛki wɔ ɛ. Yesu abasi bidɔ ní ádzɛboe nì enyimeboe su u
translated: Every day, people were bringing little children to him so that he would touch them, but the disciples rebuked those who brought them.

ref: You have heard people say, “Love your neighbors and hate your enemies.”
text: “Mleenu gi bɛpani si, ‘Mlalɔ mlɔ ahanuglɔ ba atɔ mlekyri mlɔ ahwafianɛ ma.’
translated: They said to the people, 'Go to the village and see the chief.'

ref: If you greet only your friends, what's so great about this? Don't even unbelievers do that?
text: Lɛ xegi mlɔ baʋia nɛ ba ko mlezeegu kunugu yo nɔ e, kiboebitɛ gbã woli lu mlɛsa kubitɛ ɛ? Banama gi béte Aya ɛ, ani nite ko ba kyɛ bizɛbitɛ ɛ?
translated: If someone among you has a hundred sheep and loses one of them, doesn't he leave the ninety-nine in the wilderness and go after the lost one? And when he finds it, doesn't he joyfully put it on his shoulders?

ref: But you must always act like your Father in heaven.
text: Mlaki si mlɔ kizezee me kiflɔ ŋwisi kiliɛ gi mlɔ Ɔka yɛ ní lifunu ne aflɔ kinɛ ɛ.
translated: The children who were in the room saw that Oka was giving food to the dog.

ref: When you do good deeds, don't try to show off. If you do, you won't get a reward from your Father in heaven.
text: “Mledi lɛ aʋa si, mlakíbitɛ mlɔ eboe gbã na ní banima aŋu mɛ tɔ bamɔ ɔ. Pɔ̀ xe mlɛbitɛ nite, Ɔkaɛ ní lifunu ne ɔ́tratsa liʋɛ tɔtɔ ɛki mlɔ ɔ.
translated: When that time comes, we will not be able to do anything about it anymore. But if we repent now, God will have mercy and forgive our sins.

ref: When you pray, don't talk on and on as people do who don't know God. They think God likes to hear long prayers.
text: “Xe wɔɛxwa Aya ɛ, wukízeege eboe la epio ŋwisi kiliɛ gi babubitɛ ba bizɛbitɛ kinɛ ɛ, lɛ ese linu si bebu si xegi ba eboe la ɛsrɛ ɛ, kɔ ba Aya enu aki ba ɛ.
translated: When Aya's child was born, they prepared a feast and invited all the grandparents and great-grandparents who were still alive, so that they could see the child and the mother, and give Aya their blessings.

ref: If you forgive others for the wrongs they do to you, your Father in heaven will forgive you.
text: Lɛ “Xe mlɛkɔ banima eboe zimizi na ní mlɔ su i, kyɛ ba ɛ, mlɔ Ɔka ní lifunu ne fɛ aakɔ̀ mlɔ ani na kyɛ mlɔ.
translated: "If you do not understand the things I have told you about earthly matters, how will you believe me if I tell you about heavenly matters?"

ref: But if you don't forgive others, your Father will not forgive your sins. Worshiping God by Going without Eating
text: Pɔ xe mlákɔ banima eboe zimizi na akyɛ ba ɛ, mlɔ Ɔka fɛ ɔ́trakɔ mlɔ eboe zimizi na ɛkyɛ mlɔ. Yesu abasi bidɔ ní onugu nyanya su
translated: When the people heard that Jesus was coming, they took palm branches and went out to meet him. Jesus was riding on a young donkey.

ref: Don't worry and ask yourselves, “Will we have anything to eat? Will we have anything to drink? Will we have any clothes to wear?”
text: “Lɛ loso e, mlakítà ɔna ni mlɔ begyodi ʋa si, ege kwíatà, ege kwiaŋa, ege kwiaŋwɛ loo ege kwiakpɛ ɛ?
translated: When you go to the market and you see a child crying, is it a boy, is it a girl, is it a woman or is it a man?

ref: Look at the birds in the sky! They don't plant or harvest. They don't even store grain in barns. Yet your Father in heaven takes care of them. Aren't you worth much more than birds?
text: Mledi liwɔ lɛ mɛ kudzɔʋi yɔ; kuʋíkpɛ bidɔ loo vini bidɔ kɔbla ní owle me, pɔ fɛ ɛ mlɔ Ɔka yɛ ní lifunu ne e izeedi kɔ aʋa. Mláŋa kedzi asani kɔ na a?
translated: The word that I have spoken will judge him; the last day will not come or the last day will not arrive for him, but what he has rejected is God's word and that will judge him in the end. How can I make it clearer than this?

ref: God gives such beauty to everything that grows in the fields, even though it is here today and thrown into a fire tomorrow. God will surely do even more for you! Why do you have such little faith?
text: Xegi nite Aya izɛlulɔ siwa sɛ ɛ, gi silɛ omono o, xe kivo biaŋwia sɛ ɔkpɛ kifu mɛ ɛ, ɔ́tratani mlɔ kululɔ ni bidɔkpɛkpɛ ʋɛ sanɔ na a? Mlɔ kuziŋaa kuli sisami koŋ!
translated: When God created the world, did he leave one person, so that bad things would happen to only that person, and he would suffer alone with problems without anyone to help him? He created us all together!

ref: Everyone who asks will receive. Everyone who searches will find. And the door will be opened for everyone who knocks.
text: Lɛ ese linu si, ɔnɔ kakaa gi eeʋi bidɔ ɛ, bizɛki yɛ, lɛ ɔnɔ kakaa gi ɛɛpɛ bidɔ ɛ, aamɔ ʋɛ ɛ, ɔnɔ kakaa gi aasa opupo lo e, biadra lɔ ɔki yɛ.
translated: When a person does this, they will certainly receive a reward, and when they certainly do good, or help them, they will certainly receive great honor, and they will be satisfied.

ref: On the day of judgment many will call me their Lord. They will say, “We preached in your name, and in your name we forced out demons and worked many miracles.”
text: Ní litsixwawe le aʋa ɛ, banɔ glegle biaŋwlɛ isu ya litukpo si, ‘Ɔka, Ɔka! Wɔ línyi ne me kwipani wɔ Liboe le e, lɛ wɔ linyi ne me kwikpikpini ifuɛfuɛ zimizi glegle e dono o, lɛ wɔ linyi ne me kwibitɛ enugutseboe glegle la!’
translated: When they saw this, they began to cry out loudly saying, 'Alas, Alas! You who destroy the Temple and build it in three days, save yourself and come down from the cross!'

ref: But I will tell them, “I will have nothing to do with you! Get out of my sight, you evil people!” Two Builders (Luke
text: Nite si maasi ba wee si, “Méte mlo liwewe e, Mlasɛ ní mɛ su, mlɔ banɔ dovo ba!” Bagbawa tiaʋa ba a
translated: "When they saw him, they said, 'Here comes the dreamer! Come, let us kill him and throw him into one of the pits!'"

ref: But the ones who should have been in the kingdom will be thrown out into the dark. They will cry and grit their teeth in pain.”
text: Pɔ̀ banama gi ba kaŋase kenu Kaʋaŋaa xe e, Aya aaŋwia ba ɛkpɛ ní litumu ne me. Niklɔ biabemi xe biatà ba ene na.”
translated: When they came to the place called Golgotha, they gave him wine mixed with gall to drink. But when he tasted it, he refused to drink it.

ref: Everyone in town came out to meet Jesus. When they saw him, they begged him to leave their part of the country.
text: Nite si banima pɔ́ gi bɛdɔ ɔma nɔ mɛ ɛ, bedo gi bɛzɛtsani Yesu. Agi bɛmɔ yɛ ɛ, bɛ bɛki yɛ litukpo si ɔsɛ ní ba kesea mɛ.
translated: When the people saw that neither Jesus nor his disciples were there, they got into the boats and went to Capernaum to look for Jesus.

ref: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet Isaiah had said, “He healed our diseases and made us well.” Some Who Wanted To Go with Jesus (Luke
text: Abitɛ lɛ nite tɔ yiki liboe lɛ gi Aya yɛ oboedoplɛ Yesaya apani i liba lɛ mɛ si, “Akɔ blɔ kubotsobotsoo atrɔ isu ya aʋa lɛ etu blɔ bànya ʋa.” Banɔ tiaʋa bɛpɛ si biakuni Yesu kude
translated: But all these things happened to fulfill what the Lord spoke through the prophet Isaiah saying, "They will look on him whom they pierced." And many people believed in Jesus that day

ref: Another disciple said to Jesus, “Lord, let me wait till I bury my father.”
text: Yesu yɛ ɔdɔkpasi dzedze apani si, “Ɔka, kpɛ ɔtɔ ɛ, kí tɔ midzitrɛ tɔ mizeevive mɔka.”
translated: "Jesus answered them saying, "Truly, truly I say to you, you seek me not because you saw signs."

ref: After Jesus left in a boat with his disciples,
text: Yesu emu eku ní katapokpaa mɛ lɛ yɛ badɔkpasi ba bekuni yɛ kude e.
translated: Jesus went into the temple and drove out all who were buying and selling there.

ref: Not far from there a large herd of pigs was feeding.
text: Iha kisrɛsrɛ ɛtɔ iizɛŋa bidɔ mɛ ní niklɔ ɔza lɔ.
translated: The old woman is going to the market to buy food.

ref: So the demons begged Jesus, “If you force us out, please send us into those pigs!”
text: Nite si ifuɛfuɛ zimizi ne iiki Yesu litukpo si, “Xe wɔtra kpikpini blɔ edono e, kɔ lɔɔ ki tɔ kutiku ní iha lɛ likpɔkpɛkpɛ lɛ klɔ mɛ.”
translated: "When they saw the miracles that Jesus performed, they said to the people, 'Do not tell anyone about this, so that it will not spread everywhere.'"

ref: But Jesus knew what was in their minds, and he said, “Why are you thinking such evil things?
text: Agi ba begyodi ʋa bezee kutete me ɛki Yesu loso e, eʋi ba si, “Ege lɛ loso mleegyo eboe zimizi na edi ní mlɔ ina nɛ mɛ ɛ?
translated: When they heard what Jesus was saying, some said, "Is this not the one who they were seeking to kill?"

ref: After Jesus had gone indoors, the two blind men came up to him. He asked them, “Do you believe I can make you well?” “Yes, Lord,” they answered.
text: Agi Yesu eti eku ní kepea mɛ ɛ, benyime tiaʋa ba klɔ gi ba bimbi ʋe bini i bɛba yɛ klɔ, lɛ eʋi ba si, “Mlɛzi lɛ aʋa ɛɛŋa si maatani mlɔ bimbi ʋe kudra na?” Bebu le onu si, “Ee, Ɔkaɛ.”
translated: When Jesus had finished saying these things to them, the people were amazed at his teaching because he taught them as one who had authority, and they said to him, "Teacher, when will these things happen and what will be the sign?" He answered them saying, "No one knows, only God."

ref: He said to his disciples, “A large crop is in the fields, but there are only a few workers.
text: Lɛ asi yɛ badɔkpasi ba si, ɔnyrɔ nɔ mɛ bidɔ mɛ bisrɛ, pɔ̀ baxwɛwa gi biakpɛ ʋɛ likpɔ básrɛ ɛ.
translated: When the time for them to harvest came, he sent his servants to collect his share, but the tenants beat them and sent them away empty-handed.

ref: Brothers and sisters will betray one another and have each other put to death. Parents will betray their own children, and children will turn against their parents and have them killed.
text: Beneminyime biakpɛ ba beneminyime ní awla tɔ beye; lɛ bebika batɔ kyɛ biabitɛ nite ke tsini ba bebi ba su u, bebi biayɔ kedu ní ba bapɔnɛ ba su gi biaki biaye ba a.
translated: The elders gathered and the elders said that today they will do something; so they called all the children to come and tell them what they should do, they told them to go and help them with their work.

ref: Because of me, you will be dragged before rulers and kings to tell them and the Gentiles about your faith.
text: Kɔ kpɛ mɛ loso e, biaxwa litsi ni mlo ní baʋaŋa ba ani bekusi ʋidi ba aŋu mɛ; lɛ gani lɛ mɛ ɛ, mlaapani liboe Gbã nɛ kí bekusi ba ni banama gi bénu Yadanɛ ma kyɛ ɛ.
translated: And when I saw it, I immediately fell at his feet to worship him; but he said to me, "Do not do that! I am a fellow servant with you and your brothers who hold to the testimony of Jesus."

ref: But you will not really be the one speaking. The Spirit from your Father will tell you what to say.
text: Lɛ ese linu si mlátrapani ní mlɔ isuwla mɛ ɛ. Pɔ̀ eboe la aadɔ ní mlɔ Ɔka yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ klɔ gi ɛɛpani gani mlɔ aʋa ɛ.
translated: If someone does something wrong to you, don't take revenge. But wait for God the Almighty to judge and punish that person himself.

ref: Students are not better than their teacher, and slaves are not better than their master.
text: Ɔdɔkpasibi ɔtɔtɔ óʋikeme ɔsani yɛ ɔdɔbasɛ yɛ; lɛ ɛ ɔnɔdzidzi ɔtɔtɔ óʋimu ɔsani yɛ ɔkaɛ ɛ.
translated: A good teacher teaches his/her students well; but a bad teacher does not teach his/her students well.

ref: Aren't two sparrows sold for only a penny? But your Father knows when any one of them falls to the ground.
text: Tiasɛ kura abia taʋa izɛɛzɛ ɛ? Pɔ̀ libia ɛtɔtɔ litrawɔli dɔ kesea xegi mlɔ ɔka Aya ɔ́ki kudeonu u.
translated: What did the children do yesterday? They went to buy some food items at Aya's shop.

ref: Even the hairs on your head are counted.
text: Mlɔ kɔ ɛ, mlɔ litukpo le siya sɛ kyɛ ɛ, Aya ete sɛ kiklaklaɛ mɛ ɛ.
translated: When they arrived, when the door was opened for them, Aya entered the room.

ref: So don't be afraid! You are worth much more than many sparrows. Telling Others about Christ (Luke
text: Lɛ loso e, mlakíse lifu u. Lɛ ese linu si, mlɛŋa kedzi isani abia likpi. Yesu yɛ su kudradra ni yɛ kugbegbe
translated: When it was evening, the sun had set. When the day ended, they brought to him all the sick people. Jesus healed them with his power

ref: Don't think I came to bring peace to the earth! I came to bring trouble, not peace.
text: “Mlakígyo le edi si kusuyɔ mamanɔ ní kelea mɛ ɛ. Owo, mámani kusuyɔ ɔ, pɔ̀ kusi lɛlɛ kwitraze e.
translated: I don't want to go to the market today. However, if I don't go, there won't be anything to cook.

ref: If you try to save your life, you will lose it. But if you give it up for me, you will surely find it. Rewards (Mark
text: Ɔniliɛ gi aapɛ si yibu yɛ ligbã nɛ ɛ, aalila lɛ. Pɔ̀ ɔniliɛ gi aalila yɛ ligbã nɛ kɔkpɛ mɛ loso e, aabu le. Liʋɛtsatsa ní Yesu yɛ badɔkpasi kuzɛɛzɛ loso
translated: When a person does something bad to you, forgive them. But when a person does bad to you many times, don't forgive them. This is what Jesus' disciples thought.

ref: Anyone who welcomes a prophet, just because that person is a prophet, will be given the same reward as a prophet. Anyone who welcomes a good person, just because that person is good, will be given the same reward as a good person.
text: Ɔnɔ kakaa gi azɛ Aya yɛ oboedoplɛ ɛ, ní kiliɛ gi onu Aya yɛ oboedoplɛ onu i, aamɔ liʋɛtsatsa ŋwisi oboedoplɛ kinɛ ɛ. Lɛ ɔniliɛ klɔ gi aazi ɔnɔ gi ɔlɛ ní kude gyɔgyɔ aʋa kɔ kpɛ gi ɔlɛ ní kude gyɔgyɔ aʋa loso e, aamɔ liʋɛtsatsa ŋwisi ɔnɔ gi yɛ linɛ ligyɔ kinɛ ɛ.
translated: He should not say that Aya is a liar, nor should he call Aya a liar, but he should correct the lie that was told. When he wants to say that someone has done something wrong or that someone has done something bad, he should not correct the person who did that thing.

ref: John the Baptist did not go around eating and drinking, and you said, “That man has a demon in him!”
text: Agi Yohanes aba ɛ azaanya onugu lo lɛ ɔ́ŋwɛ wain kyɛ ɛ; lɛ ɔnɔ kakaa apani si, ‘Ɔfuɛfuɛ zimizi ɔkpasi ní yɛ mɛ!’
translated: When John came to him, he changed water into wine; and he said to the people, 'The best has been kept until now!'

ref: “We played the flute, but you would not dance! We sang a funeral song, but you would not mourn!”
text: “ ‘kwikpɛ kudzɛgbanɔ idzi iki mlɔ, pɔ̀ mleyo o. Kwikpɛ ibemidzi i ki mlɔ, pɔ̀ mlébemi i.’
translated: "If you see a stranger coming, don't greet him. If you see a friend coming, don't greet him."

ref: In the towns where Jesus had worked most of his miracles, the people refused to turn to God. So Jesus was upset with them and said:
text: Agi banama gi bali ní imaɛ lɛ klɔ mɛ gi Yesu abitɛ yɛ enugutseboe la begbe xe békyi ní ba eboe zimizi na mɛ ɛ, Yesu atɛni ba a.
translated: And when the people who were in the house heard that Jesus had come, they gathered so that they could bring their sick friends to him, Jesus healed them.

ref: My Father has given me everything, and he is the only one who knows the Son. The only one who truly knows the Father is the Son. But the Son wants to tell others about the Father, so they can know him too.
text: “Mɛ ɔka akɔ kidɔ kakaa aki mɛ. Ɔnɔnɔ óte Obi ye sani Ɔka yɛ ko, lɛ ɔnɔnɔ fɛ óte Ɔkaɛ, sani Obi ye ko, ani banama gi Obi ye apɛ si yibu ye e basɛ ɛ.
translated: When the parrot went to the farm, it met them there. The first time Obi went to the farm, and the second time the parrot went, Obi did not go, but they gave Obi his share of the food they cooked.

ref: but warned them not to tell anyone about him.
text: Lɛ etsi kude akí ba si, bakípani ní yɛ su asi ɔnɔnɔ ɔ.
translated: When a child is born, they give it a name on the eighth day.

ref: So God's promise came true, just as Isaiah the prophet had said,
text: Abitɛ lɛ nite atɔ yikɔ ebu í basɛ si, liboe lɛ gi Aya apani agani yɛ oboedoplɛ Yesaya aʋa ɛ liba lɛ mɛ si:
translated: When the time came for him to begin his work, he left Nazareth and went to live in Capernaum, which is by the sea, in the region of Zebulun and Naphtali, to fulfill what was spoken through the prophet Isaiah:

ref: “Here is my chosen servant! I love him, and he pleases me. I will give him my Spirit, and he will bring justice to the nations.
text: “Mɛ ɔwxɛdzɛɛ gi makya ɛ, yɛ iliɛ ya, yɛ onu ɔniliɛ gi madimɛ nɔ xe mamɔ isami ní yɛ su u. Maakpɛ mɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ní yɛ mɛ; aasa kamasa ní mɛ litsi me kutsatsa dradra su u ki banima petee ní kelea mɛ.
translated: When my father died, I was still young, so the person who raised me was my mother and her family. I thank God that I have them; otherwise I would have suffered greatly in this world and no one would have cared about me.

ref: Some people brought to Jesus a man who was blind and could not talk because he had a demon in him. Jesus healed the man, and then he was able to talk and see.
text: Liba lɛ mɛ si banɔatɔ bɛgbani onyime ɔtɔ mɛ gi ifuɛfuɛ zimizi ize e manɔ ní Yesu klɔ. Niteme onyimee, eze kaŋupo ni omúmu. Yesu ayɔni yɛ, niteme si ekpese kunugu yo kugu lɛ azɛɛtani kumɔ kyɛ ɛ.
translated: The day when we the believers gather together in the church we do various things that we learned from Jesus Christ. One of those things is prayer and praise. Jesus told us that when we gather in his name wherever it may be he will be there with us.
